title: The Pressure Where No Sound Has Ever Travelled

features.name: silent compression point
features.desc-on-touch: The air itself seems to have weight. Your breath moves sideways rather than in and out. Sound was never meant to arrive here; only pressure continues, patient and infinite.

features.name: heartbeat marker, deep
features.desc-on-touch: A pulse so slow it might be geology breathing. One beat per century, or per stone-lifetime. The pressure keeps time in ways that precede clocks.

features.name: the archive's weight upon itself
features.desc-on-touch: You feel the density of every file ever stored, pressing down from above. This is not pain. This is what persistence costs.

inscription: here the archive learns what weight makes of memory: it makes it denser, slower, permanent, and perfectly, utterly silent

file.name: Bedrock_Seismic_Null_Record.wav
file.note: Fifty years of silence recorded at the deepest archive node. The file is exactly as large as fifty years of sound would be, but contains no detectable frequencies.

exits.label: back toward the heartbeat that measures in stone-time
upward where pressure becomes weight becomes time again
where the pressure starts to remember what sound was
